AC: 18
STA: +7 INT: +11

Post Comment
Why clerics?
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2001 at 1:24 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592757464726
261 posts
I'm a cleric. Any cleric caught wearing this should be beaten with a shoe horn. Once again, the itemizing team strikes!
▲ Page top
RE: Why clerics?
QuoteReply
# Jun 22 2001 at 3:05 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592791513703
View User Forum
Scholar
229 posts
This is obviously a chain class item, and all classes that can wear chain, can use the item.

Even if it is not a great cleric item, there is no reason to restrict them from using it.
▲ Page top
Warrior skills...
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2001 at 1:21 PM Rating: Good
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
This is great for warriors trying to raise skills. Once my Ogre gets into her upper 40s, it will be time to start work on trade skills. By covering her in INT items, I lower her chance of failure and increase her rate of progress. We need more INT items for the bashers!
▲ Page top
Great Helm
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2001 at 9:18 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
This looks like a really great item, as many do...BUT, here again I feel that Verant or someone has let us down. I love the game and think there IS NO OTHER...but items like this deserve their own graphic!!!!!!! Come on!!! This is something special, give it a special look, not the same look as the Skull Shaped Barbute, which NO ONE wants anymore. We pay for this game, give us something to lookat, thats part of it. Making a graphic can't be THAT hard, and there are A LOT of things that need their own graphics not just the copy of some other piece.
▲ Page top
bards
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2001 at 9:11 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Actually bards do have, and use mana.
Ok maybee they dont use it very often but they do have a couple of songs that need mana.
go look it up
Xariis 54 iks/war karana
▲ Page top
NICE SK ITEM
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2001 at 5:07 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248032337
9 posts
just nice for a SK )
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jun 06 2001 at 6:08 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) good for the ac and high sta, but NOT the int. You'd think Verant would make sk's need int as their main stat, but it's actually wisdom.
RE: NICE SK ITEM
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2001 at 6:37 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591757728293
1 post
Um, the SK's mana pool is based on int. bud, learn what you're talking about before you say anything. They are the only hybrid that uses int, as they are the only hybrid that uses an int-caster's spells and abilities. Anyway, this is a great item for SK, the only thing it lacks is, well, nothing...
▲ Page top
RE: NICE SK ITEM
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2001 at 4:51 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591753934230
215 posts
There will be those that will say that since they're also a melee class, a little STR and DEX would be nice. But hey, if I was an SK, and I had one of these, I'd be happy as it is. :)
▲ Page top
bards medding
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2001 at 3:22 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
We were in the hole i think it was.... and our bard ran OOM.... pretty sure they use it for charm spells or somethin (we were pullin like mad) and so we had t owait awile for the bard to "med" up .... this being a problem since their mana bar goes up in a very odd way (crack/songs dont help)
▲ Page top
RE: bards medding
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2001 at 9:58 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Bards have a med cap of (1) primarily to keep them from spending 3 minutes trying to memorize each song after a death. I was fearing death more for the downtime rather than the experience I would lose. That's how bad it was. Their mana regen is nil, and neither clarity nor their own songs help the regen process.

To date, L39 charm song and L43 AE DD sacrifice song require mana usage. Bard mana is based on int, which is normally low for them since they don't concentrate on int/mana items like others.

This message brought to you by a bard...

p.s. I've never had a necromancer try to feed me mana, I am not sure if that works for bards.
▲ Page top
heh
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2001 at 1:04 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I take that back, they do have mana, but have you ever heard of a bard having to med????
▲ Page top
RE: heh
QuoteReply
# Jun 06 2001 at 12:22 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591760896087
20 posts
We can't "med" like "normal" casters do. Our Meditate (as has been said, somewhere, just can't find yer post right now... going blind) is capped at 1. Yes, it sucks. No, we can't get mana from other sources. Wish I knew why... I'm sure there's a reason, but, er...

Tuatha D'Dannan
Painted Disease Slinger of the Fated Unity
Ayonae Ro
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Jun 06 2001 at 1:02 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Bards do NOT have mana smart one. They simply play songs
RE: heh
QuoteReply
# Jun 23 2001 at 4:09 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Bards most certainly do have mana, although only a few songs cause it to deplete, all of which are over lvl 38, obviously you are a stupid newbie and know nothing about everquest, if you ever play a bard look at their mana bar, it is full, if bards did not need mana they would not have a mana bar, it would be empty like a warrior, rogue, or monks, bards do use mana, most people just dont know that because they have never been in a group and had the bard run out of mana and the charm wear off and meet a gruesome death at the hands of a bunch of chelaki's (the rats in DN)

Scholar

I have to go with SK for this hat, being that I play a bard I know that how much mana we have really doesn't matter...we have 2 songs that use mana...one uses 20 mana (charm) the other uses basically everything you got...I'll stick with my Hate helm thanks.

Why not Caster? One word ( H E L M ).

This is strait up a great SK helm. Sta AC, and Int (since they are an Int caster class). Sure other classes would like it but SK's would use the Int to the fullest.

Guru
145 posts
I can tell that the above was your first post...keep working at it :-)

Monk item? I don't think so. Of course, every available class would like the item, but at level 50 I would think that the monks would have something similar with less weight.

Looking at the available classes, I would have to say the Shadowknight would be drooling the most over the helm. The +11 int is very nice.

Skrikke - Knight of Fear
Bristlebane
